title: Wall Chart B – Management of Seizures
learningObjectives: Identify the appropriate management steps for seizures in neonates from birth to 2 weeks of age.
Calculate the correct phenobarbitone dose (20 mg/kg) and adjust volume based on the vial concentration.
Describe the management protocol for seizures in infants older than 2 weeks.
Reference related topics such as general convulsion management.

sections.title: Infant from birth up to 2 weeks of age with seizures

image.altText: Seizure management chart for infants 0‑2 weeks

notes: The volumes in the chart assume phenobarbitone is supplied as a 200 mg/mL preparation.
The recommended dose is 20 mg/kg; recalculate the volume if a different concentration is used.

sections.title: Managing seizures beyond 2 weeks of age

image.altText: Seizure management chart for infants >2 weeks

notes: The volumes in the chart assume phenobarbitone is supplied as a 200 mg/mL preparation.
The recommended dose is 20 mg/kg; recalculate the volume if a different concentration is used.
